volumeTitle: Birds. Lysistrata. Women at the Thesmophoria
label: Birds (Section 580 - Section 670)

sections.label: Section 580
sections.original: κἄπειτʼ αὐτοῖς ἡ Δημήτηρ πυροὺς πεινῶσι μετρείτω .
sections.translation: we shall see then if Demeter will mete them out any wheat .

sections.label: Section 581
sections.original: οὐκ ἐθελήσει μὰ Δίʼ , ἀλλʼ ὄψει προφάσεις αὐτὴν παρέχουσαν .
sections.translation: By Zeus , she ' ll take good care she does not , and you will see her inventing a thousand excuses .

sections.label: Section 582
sections.original: οἱ δʼ αὖ κόρακες τῶν ζευγαρίων , οἷσιν τὴν γῆν καταροῦσιν ,
sections.translation: The crows too will prove your divinity to them by pecking out the eyes of their flocks and of their draught-oxen ; and then let Apollo cure them , since he is a physician and is paid for the purpose .

sections.label: Section 585
sections.original: μὴ πρίν γʼ ἂν ἐγὼ τὼ βοιδαρίω τὠμὼ πρώτιστʼ ἀποδῶμαι .
sections.translation: Oh ! don ' t do that ! Wait first until I have sold my two young bullocks .

sections.label: Section 586
sections.original: ἢν δʼ ἡγῶνται σὲ θεὸν σὲ βίον σὲ δὲ γῆν σὲ Κρόνον σὲ Ποσειδῶ ,
sections.translation: If on the other hand they recognize that you are God , the principle of life , that you are Earth , Kronos , Poseidon , they shall be loaded with benefits .

sections.label: Section 587b
sections.original: λέγε δή μοι τῶν ἀγαθῶν ἕν .
sections.translation: Name me one of these then .

sections.label: Section 588
sections.original: πρῶτα μὲν αὐτῶν τὰς οἰνάνθας οἱ πάρνοπες οὐ κατέδονται ,
sections.translation: Firstly , the locusts shall not eat up their vine-blossoms ; a legion of owls and kestrels will devour them .

sections.label: Section 590
sections.original: εἶθʼ οἱ κνῖπες καὶ ψῆνες ἀεὶ τὰς συκᾶς οὐ κατέδονται ,
sections.translation: Moreover , the gnats and the gallbugs shall no longer ravage the figs ; a flock of thrushes shall swallow the whole host down to the very last .

sections.label: Section 592
sections.original: πλουτεῖν δὲ πόθεν δώσομεν αὐτοῖς ; καὶ γὰρ τούτου σφόδρʼ ἐρῶσιν .
sections.translation: And how shall we give wealth to mankind ? This is their strongest passion .

sections.label: Section 593
sections.original: τὰ μέταλλʼ αὐτοῖς μαντευομένοις οὗτοι δώσουσι τὰ χρηστά ,
sections.translation: When they consult the omens , you will point them to the richest mines , you will reveal the paying ventures to the diviner ,

sections.label: Section 595
sections.original: ὥστʼ ἀπολεῖται τῶν ναυκλήρων οὐδείς .
sections.translation: and not another shipwreck will happen or sailor perish .

sections.label: Section 595b
sections.original: πῶς οὐκ ἀπολεῖται ;
sections.translation: No more shall perish ? How is that ?

sections.label: Section 596
sections.original: προερεῖ τις ἀεὶ τῶν ὀρνίθων μαντευομένῳ περὶ τοῦ πλοῦ ·
sections.translation: When the auguries are examined before starting on a voyage , some bird will not fail to say , Don ' t start ! there will be a storm , or else , Go ! you will make a most profitable venture .

sections.label: Section 598
sections.original: γαῦλον κτῶμαι καὶ ναυκληρῶ , κοὐκ ἂν μείναιμι παρʼ ὑμῖν .
sections.translation: I shall buy a trading-vessel and go to sea . I will not stay with you .

sections.label: Section 599
sections.original: τοὺς θησαυρούς τʼ αὐτοῖς δείξουσʼ οὓς οἱ πρότεροι κατέθεντο
sections.translation: You will discover treasures to them , which were buried in former times ,

sections.label: Section 600
sections.original: τῶν ἀργυρίων · οὗτοι γὰρ ἴσασι · λέγουσι δέ τοι τάδε πάντες ,
sections.translation: for you know them . Do not all men say , None knows where my treasure lies , unless perchance it be some bird .

sections.label: Section 602
sections.original: πωλῶ γαῦλον , κτῶμαι σμινύην , καὶ τὰς ὑδρίας ἀνορύττω .
sections.translation: I shall sell my boat and buy a spade to unearth the vessels .

sections.label: Section 603
sections.original: πῶς δʼ ὑγιείαν δώσουσʼ αὐτοῖς , οὖσαν παρὰ τοῖσι θεοῖσιν ;
sections.translation: And how are we to give them health , which belongs to the gods ?

sections.label: Section 604
sections.original: ἢν εὖ πράττωσʼ , οὐχ ὑγιεία μεγάλη τοῦτʼ ἐστί ; σάφʼ ἴσθι ,
sections.translation: If they are happy , is not that the chief thing towards health ?

sections.label: Section 605
sections.original: ὡς ἄνθρωπός γε κακῶς πράττων ἀτεχνῶς οὐδεὶς ὑγιαίνει .
sections.translation: The miserable man is never well .

sections.label: Section 606
sections.original: πῶς δʼ ἐς γῆράς ποτʼ ἀφίξονται ; καὶ γὰρ τοῦτʼ ἔστʼ ἐν Ὀλύμπῳ ·
sections.translation: Old Age also dwells in Olympus . How will they get at it ? Must they die in early youth ?

sections.label: Section 607b
sections.original: μὰ Δίʼ ἀλλὰ τριακόσιʼ αὐτοῖς
sections.translation: Why , the birds , by Zeus ,

sections.label: Section 608
sections.original: ἔτι προσθήσουσʼ ὄρνιθες ἔτη .
sections.translation: will add three hundred years to their life .

sections.label: Section 608b
sections.original: παρὰ τοῦ ;
sections.translation: From whom will they take them ?

sections.label: Section 608c
sections.original: παρʼ ὅτου ; παρʼ ἑαυτῶν .
sections.translation: From whom ? Why , from themselves . Don ' t you know the cawing crow lives five times as long as a man ?

sections.label: Section 610
sections.original: αἰβοῖ πολλῷ κρείττους οὗτοι τοῦ Διὸς ἡμῖν βασιλεύειν .
sections.translation: Ah ! ah ! these are far better kings for us than Zeus !

sections.label: Section 611
sections.original: οὐ γὰρ πολλῷ ;
sections.translation: Far better , are they not ? And firstly , we shall not have to build them temples of hewn stone , closed with gates of gold ;

sections.label: Section 615
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ὑπὸ θάμνοις καὶ πρινιδίοις
sections.translation: they will dwell amongst the bushes and in the thickets of green oak ; the most venerated of birds will have no other temple than the foliage of the olive tree ; we shall not go to Delphi or to Ammon

sections.label: Section 620
sections.original: θύσομεν , ἀλλʼ ἐν ταῖσιν κομάροις
sections.translation: to sacrifice ; but standing erect in the midst of arbutus and wild olives and holding forth our hands filled with wheat and barley , we shall pray them to admit us to a share of the blessings they enjoy

sections.label: Section 625
sections.original: παραχρῆμʼ ἔσται
sections.translation: and shall at once obtain them for a few grains of wheat .

sections.label: Section 627
sections.original: ὦ φίλτατʼ ἐμοὶ πολὺ πρεσβυτῶν ἐξ ἐχθίστου μεταπίπτων ,
sections.translation: Old man , whom I detested , you are now to me the dearest of all ; never shall I , if I can help it , fail to follow your advice .

sections.label: Section 629
sections.original: ἐπαυχήσας δὲ τοῖσι σοῖς λόγοις
sections.translation: Inspirited by your words ,

sections.label: Section 630
sections.original: ἐπηπείλησα καὶ κατώμοσα ,
sections.translation: I threaten my rivals the gods , and I swear that if you march in alliance with me against the gods and are faithful to our just , loyal and sacred bond , we shall soon have shattered their scepter .

sections.label: Section 637
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ὅσα μὲν δεῖ ῥώμῃ πράττειν , ἐπὶ ταῦτα τεταξόμεθʼ ἡμεῖς ·
sections.translation: We shall charge ourselves with the performance of everything that requires force ; that which demands thought and deliberation shall be yours to supply .

sections.label: Section 639
sections.original: καὶ μὴν μὰ τὸν Δίʼ οὐχὶ νυστάζειν ἔτι
sections.translation: By Zeus ! it ' s no longer

sections.label: Section 640
sections.original: ὥρα ʼστὶν ἡμῖν οὐδὲ μελλονικιᾶν ,
sections.translation: the time to delay and loiter like Nicias ; let us act as promptly as possible .... In the first place , come , enter my nest built of brushwood and blades of straw , and tell me your names .

sections.label: Section 643b
sections.original: ἀλλὰ ῥᾴδιον .
sections.translation: That is soon done ; my name is Pisthetaerus , and his ,

sections.label: Section 645
sections.original: Εὐελπίδης Κριῶθεν .
sections.translation: Euelpides , of the deme Crioa .

sections.label: Section 645b
sections.original: ἀλλὰ χαίρετον
sections.translation: Good ! and good luck to you .

sections.label: Section 646b
sections.original: δεχόμεθα .
sections.translation: We accept the omen .

sections.label: Section 646c
sections.original: δεῦρο τοίνυν εἴσιτον .
sections.translation: Come in here .

sections.label: Section 647
sections.original: ἴωμεν · εἰσηγοῦ σὺ λαβὼν ἡμᾶς .
sections.translation: Very well , you are the one who must lead us and introduce us .

sections.label: Section 647b
sections.original: ἴθι .
sections.translation: Come then .

sections.label: Section 648
sections.original: ἀτὰρ τὸ δεῖνα , δεῦρʼ ἐπανάκρουσαι πάλιν .
sections.translation: Oh ! my god ! do come back here . Hi ! tell us how

sections.label: Section 650
sections.original: ξυνεσόμεθʼ ὑμῖν πετομένοις οὐ πετομένω ;
sections.translation: we are to follow you . You can fly , but we cannot .

sections.label: Section 651
sections.original: καλῶς .
sections.translation: Well , well .

sections.label: Section 651b
sections.original: ὅρα νυν , ὡς ἐν Αἰσώπου λόγοις
sections.translation: Remember Aesop ' s fables . It is told there that the fox fared very badly , because he had made an alliance with the eagle .

sections.label: Section 654
sections.original: μηδὲν φοβηθῇς · ἔστι γάρ τι ῥιζίον ,
sections.translation: Be at ease . You shall eat a certain root

sections.label: Section 655
sections.original: ὃ διατραγόντʼ ἔσεσθον ἐπτερωμένω .
sections.translation: and wings will grow on your shoulders .

sections.label: Section 656
sections.original: οὕτω μὲν εἰσίωμεν . ἄγε δὴ Ξανθία
sections.translation: Then let us enter . Xanthias and Manodorus , pick up our baggage .

sections.label: Section 658
sections.original: οὗτος σὲ καλῶ , σὲ λέγω .
sections.translation: Hi ! Epops ! do you hear me ?

sections.label: Section 658b
sections.original: τί καλεῖς ;
sections.translation: What ' s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 658c
sections.original: τούτους μὲν ἄγων μετὰ σαυτοῦ
sections.translation: Take them off to dine well and call your mate , the melodious Procne , whose songs are worthy of the Muses ;

sections.label: Section 660
sections.original: κατάλειφʼ ἡμῖν δεῦρʼ ἐκβιβάσας , ἵνα παίσωμεν μετʼ ἐκείνης .
sections.translation: she will delight our leisure moments .

sections.label: Section 661
sections.original: ὦ τοῦτο μεντοι νὴ Δίʼ αὐτοῖσιν πιθοῦ ·
sections.translation: Oh ! I conjure you , accede to their wish ; for this delightful bird will leave her rushes at the sound of your voice ;

sections.label: Section 663
sections.original: ἐκβίβασον αὐτοῦ πρὸς θεῶν αὐτήν , ἵνα
sections.translation: for the sake of the gods , let her come here , so that we may contemplate the nightingale .

sections.label: Section 665
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἰ δοκεῖ σφῷν , ταῦτα χρὴ δρᾶν . ἡ Πρόκνη
sections.translation: Let it be as you desire . Come forth , Procne , show yourself to these strangers .

sections.label: Section 667
sections.original: ὦ Ζεῦ πολυτίμηθʼ ὡς καλὸν τοὐρνίθιον ,
sections.translation: Oh ! great Zeus ! what a beautiful little bird ! what a dainty form ! what brilliant plumage !

sections.label: Section 668b
sections.original: ἆρά γʼ οἶσθʼ ὅτι
sections.translation: Do you know how dearly I should like to get between her thighs ?

sections.label: Section 670
sections.original: ὅσον δʼ ἔχει τὸν χρυσόν , ὥσπερ παρθένος .
sections.translation: She is dazzling all over with gold , like a young girl .
